title: Add reverse map for O(1) container untrack in SessionManager
description: BAD-1: session.rs untrack_container iterates ALL sessions O(n) to find the container. Add HashMap<String,String> mapping container_id -> session_id for O(1) untrack.
close_reason: done

title: REFACTOR: Extract rewrite.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract response body rewriting into \`src/rewrite.rs\`.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`rewrite_response_body()\` (lines 672-712)

**Dependencies:**
- Uses \`normalize_versioned_path()\` — import from \`crate::paths\`.

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod rewrite;\`

**Target file size:** ~50 lines.
close_reason: Extracted rewrite.rs (45 lines)

title: Add missing security tests: chunked body, session ID, UsernsMode
description: Add tests for: (1) oversized body rejection without Content-Length, (2) malicious session ID sanitization, (3) UsernsMode:host and CgroupnsMode:host policy denial. These are the most critical security features with zero test coverage.
close_reason: done

title: SEC: Add backend request timeouts
description:
`BackendClient::send()` has no timeout. If Podman hangs, the proxy hangs.
Connections back up, file descriptors exhaust, proxy becomes unresponsive.

**Fix:**
- Add a connect timeout (default: 5s) and request timeout (default: 30s) to
  both the reqwest and hyper clients.
- For reqwest: use `.timeout()` and `.connect_timeout()` on the builder.
- For hyper/Unix: wrap the request future in `tokio::time::timeout()`.
- Add `PSP_BACKEND_TIMEOUT_SECS` env var to Config (single value, controls
  the overall request timeout).
- Return 504 Gateway Timeout (not 502) when the backend times out.
  Add a `Timeout` variant to `ProxyError` if doing this after error.rs extraction.

**Files:** `BackendClient` in `src/lib.rs` (or `src/backend.rs` after extraction).
**No dependencies on other issues — can be done independently.**
close_reason: Added BACKEND_TIMEOUT (300s) to both reqwest and hyper unix clients

title: Fix json_response panic in error handler
description: BAD-7: error.rs json_response uses .expect() which panics in the error path. Return a plain-text 500 fallback if serialization or response building fails.
close_reason: done

title: REFACTOR: Extract backend.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract the backend HTTP/Unix client into \`src/backend.rs\`.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`BackendClient\` enum + impl (lines 156-263)
- \`ForwardedResponse\` struct (lines 265-269)

**Cleanup while extracting:**
- The Http and Unix arms of \`send()\` are ~40 lines each and 90% identical.
  Factor the shared header-copying logic into a helper, or at minimum add a
  comment acknowledging the duplication and why it exists (different client types
  make a trait awkward here).
- Build the startup_sweep filter query from constants instead of hardcoding the
  URL-encoded JSON blob. Use \`session::LABEL_MANAGED\` and \`serde_json\` to
  build the filter, then URL-encode it with \`url::form_urlencoded\`.
  The current magic string is at \`AppState::startup_sweep()\` line 123.

**Dependencies within the moved code:**
- \`BackendClient\` uses \`ProxyError\` — import from \`crate::error\`.
- \`AppState\` methods (\`startup_sweep\`, \`cleanup_tracked_resources\`) call
  \`BackendClient\` methods — they stay wherever AppState lives (lib.rs or
  a new state.rs if needed).

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod backend;\`

**Target file size:** ~150 lines.
close_reason: Extracted backend.rs (142 lines) with shared header filtering and proper URL encoding for sweep

title: REFACTOR: Extract paths.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract path utilities into \`src/paths.rs\`. Also fix two bugs in this code.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`normalize_versioned_path()\` (lines 561-574)
- \`is_supported_endpoint()\` (lines 520-559)
- \`path_segment_count()\` (lines 576-581)
- \`container_id_from_path()\` (lines 663-670)
- \`hop_by_hop_header()\` (lines 507-518)

**Bug fixes to include:**
1. \`hop_by_hop_header()\`: Remove the \`.to_ascii_lowercase()\` call —
   \`HeaderName::as_str()\` already returns lowercase. Unnecessary allocation
   on every header of every response.
2. \`is_supported_endpoint()\`: The pattern \`normalized.ends_with("/json")\`
   would match \`/containers/foo/bar/json\`. Add a segment count check
   (should be exactly 3: \`containers/{id}/json\`), similar to how DELETE
   already checks \`path_segment_count == 2\`.

**Consolidation:**
- Merge \`container_id_from_path()\` and \`extract_target_container()\` (from
  the audit code) into a single function. They do the same thing differently.
  Keep the one that returns \`Option<&str>\` and adapt callers.

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod paths;\`

**Target file size:** ~80 lines.
**Test impact:** \`accepts_versioned_and_unversioned_supported_paths\` test
should move to \`tests/\` later (TEST-2 issue).
**No dependencies on other refactoring issues.**
close_reason: Extracted paths.rs with hop_by_hop fix and is_supported_endpoint segment count fix

title: Build PSP (Podman Socket Proxy): policy-gated Testcontainers runtime for sandboxed agents
description:
Objective
Create PSP as a standalone project that enables Testcontainers usage from sandboxed agents without exposing raw host Podman socket access.

Scope
- Docker-compatible broker API fronting rootless Podman
- Policy enforcement and deny-by-default posture
- Session isolation, cleanup, and auditability
- Clear integration contract for AGS

Success Criteria
- Agents can run integration tests through PSP endpoint
- Dangerous container operations are blocked by policy
- Lifecycle/cleanup is deterministic
- AGS integration requires only minimal wiring

Out of Scope
- Multi-tenant auth system
- Managed cloud service deployment
- Non-Linux runtime parity beyond explicit docs
close_reason: PSP ADR, broker, policy engine, host/port resolution, lifecycle cleanup, audit logging, tests, and docs completed

title: Write ADR + threat model for PSP trust boundaries
description:
Goal
Lock architecture and threat model before implementation.

Deliverables
- ADR describing boundaries: sandbox process, PSP, host Podman API
- Abuse case matrix and mitigations
- Rationale for broker vs raw socket mounting

Acceptance Criteria
- ADR approved and actionable for implementation tasks
- Residual risks explicitly documented
close_reason: ADR and threat model documented in docs/adr/0001-psp-architecture.md

title: Implement PSP MVP Docker-compatible API broker
description:
Goal
Implement core Docker-compatible API broker to satisfy Testcontainers base lifecycle operations.

Scope
- Unix socket listener
- Forward/translate required endpoints to rootless Podman API
- Consistent error mapping for client compatibility

Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end create/start/inspect/logs/wait/remove works through PSP
- Unsupported endpoints fail with explicit structured errors
close_reason: Implemented Unix-socket PSP broker with endpoint allowlist, backend forwarding, and end-to-end lifecycle tests

title: Add PSP policy engine (deny by default)
description:
Goal
Prevent privilege escalation and unsafe runtime operations.

Policy v1
- deny privileged mode and host namespaces
- deny unrestricted bind mounts (allowlist only)
- deny device mounts and dangerous capability adds
- optional image allow/deny controls

Acceptance Criteria
- Policies are schema-validated and version controlled
- Denials include stable rule IDs and human-readable reasons
- Unit tests cover allow/deny matrix
close_reason: Added schema-validated deny-by-default policy engine with stable rule IDs and allow/deny tests

title: Define Testcontainers compatibility profile and endpoint contract
description:
Goal
Define and document exactly which Docker API features PSP supports for Testcontainers.

Scope
- Identify API calls used by Rust testcontainers flows
- Build compatibility matrix (required, optional, unsupported)
- Document behavior differences from Docker Engine

Acceptance Criteria
- Compatibility profile published in docs
- Contract tests prove required endpoints work
close_reason: Published PSP Testcontainers compatibility profile and added endpoint contract coverage

title: Implement host/port resolution strategy for in-container test clients
description:
Goal
Ensure tests running in sandbox can reliably reach published service ports.

Scope
- Resolve host mapping strategy for Podman-backed runtime
- Validate random host ports and parallel test execution
- Provide deterministic defaults and override knobs

Acceptance Criteria
- Reproducible test shows reliable connectivity from sandbox test process
- Troubleshooting guidance for hostname/port failures exists
close_reason: Implemented advertised-host inspect rewriting with reproducible connectivity test and troubleshooting guide

title: Implement PSP session lifecycle and orphan cleanup
description:
Goal
Track and clean all resources created via PSP across normal and abnormal exits.

Scope
- Session labeling for containers/networks/volumes
- Cleanup on normal stop and crash-recovery sweep
- Optional keep-on-failure debug mode

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated runs do not leak resources
- Crash recovery verified by integration test
close_reason: Added session labels, startup stale-resource sweep, cleanup, and crash-recovery coverage

title: Add structured audit logs and deny diagnostics
description:
Goal
Make policy decisions observable and debuggable.

Scope
- Structured allow/deny logs with rule IDs
- Context fields: session, operation, target image/resource
- Secret-safe logging/redaction

Acceptance Criteria
- Operators can trace blocked requests to specific policy rules
- Logs are parseable and documented
close_reason: Added structured allow/deny audit logs, safe request summaries, and documented deny diagnostics

title: Create PSP integration and compatibility test suite
description:
Goal
Validate PSP behavior against real container lifecycle and testcontainers-like flows.

Scope
- Happy path and blocked path tests
- Parallel-run stress tests
- Resource leak assertions

Acceptance Criteria
- CI/local target runs deterministic test suite
- Failures provide clear diagnostics
close_reason: Added deterministic integration suite covering happy path, blocked path, parallel inspect, and cleanup behavior

title: Publish PSP docs: architecture, policy, operations, AGS integration contract
description:
Goal
Document PSP for users and integrators.

Scope
- Architecture and threat model
- Policy file reference and examples
- Operational runbook and troubleshooting
- AGS integration contract/versioning notes

Acceptance Criteria
- New contributor can run and validate PSP from docs alone
close_reason: Published architecture, policy, operations, and AGS integration documentation

title: SEC: Improve image reference matching in policy
description:
Image allowlist/denylist in `policy.rs:156-174` uses exact string comparison.
This means:
- \`postgres:16\` in allowlist won't match \`docker.io/library/postgres:16\`
- \`postgres:16\` won't match \`postgres:16.2\`
- Digest references (\`postgres@sha256:...\`) are never matched

**Fix:**
- Normalize image references before comparison: strip \`docker.io/library/\`
  prefix, handle default tag \`:latest\`.
- Compare name and tag separately. Consider supporting prefix/glob matching
  for tags (e.g., \`postgres:16*\` matches \`postgres:16.2\`).
- At minimum, normalize both the policy entry and the request image ref to a
  canonical form before comparing.
- Add tests for: fully qualified refs, default registry, default tag, digest
  refs, tag prefix matching.

**Files:** \`src/policy.rs\` only.
**No dependencies on other issues.**
close_reason: Added normalize_image_ref() for fully-qualified image comparison

title: Improve PSP UX, discovery, and configuration ergonomics
description:
Objective
Make PSP easier to start, understand, and operate without weakening its deny-by-default security posture.

Scope
- Guided operator CLI and readiness checks
- Better failure remediation and policy authoring UX
- Discovery workflows for existing containers and image allowlisting
- Layered config resolution for user and project contexts

Success Criteria
- Operators can validate PSP setup before first request
- Integrators can see effective config and diagnose missing session wiring quickly
- Pre-existing containers and allowed images can be discovered through explicit, audited workflows
- Config can be resolved from global and project scopes, including git worktrees

Out of Scope
- Broad Docker API parity beyond the documented compatibility profile
- Implicit access to host containers without explicit allow/deny policy and audit coverage

title: Add PSP CLI commands for run, doctor, config, and version
description:
Goal
Wrap PSP in a product-shaped CLI so operators do not need to memorize environment variables.

Acceptance Criteria
- `psp run` starts the proxy using the same core server path as today
- `psp doctor` validates backend reachability, socket paths, and policy loading
- `psp config show` prints effective resolved configuration without secrets
- `psp version` prints build/version metadata
- CLI help clearly documents subcommands and key flags

title: Implement layered PSP config resolution with global and project scopes
description:
Goal
Let PSP resolve configuration from user-level and project-level files before applying environment overrides.

Acceptance Criteria
- PSP reads global config from `~/.config/psp` (document exact filename/format)
- PSP walks upward from the current directory to detect the project root
- Git worktrees are handled correctly by resolving the real repository root where `.git` lives or points
- Project config is loaded from the repository root and merged predictably with global config
- Environment variables remain the highest-precedence override
- Docs describe precedence, merge behavior, and examples

title: Improve PSP startup readiness checks and ready-state output
description:
Goal
Make PSP startup feel trustworthy by validating dependencies before declaring readiness.

Acceptance Criteria
- PSP does not log or print a ready/listening message until policy load, backend checks, and startup sweep have succeeded
- Startup failures identify the failing dependency clearly
- Ready output includes listen socket, backend, policy source, advertised host, and active compatibility profile
- `psp doctor` reuses the same validation logic where practical

title: Enrich PSP deny and unsupported-endpoint responses with remediation hints
description:
Goal
Help users recover from blocked requests faster by pairing denials with actionable guidance.

Acceptance Criteria
- Policy denials include optional remediation metadata such as `hint` and docs references
- Unsupported-endpoint responses point users to the compatibility profile docs
- PSP emits a request identifier or similar correlation handle that can be matched to logs
- Response changes remain explicit in docs and integration tests

title: Parse container create body once, pass parsed Value downstream
description: BAD-3: proxy.rs parses the same JSON body 3 times (audit, policy, label injection). Parse once, pass the parsed serde_json::Value to audit::from_request, policy::evaluate_container_create, and session::inject_session_labels. Requires signature changes in all three.
close_reason: done

title: TEST: Create shared test helpers and consolidate all tests
description:
Create \`tests/helpers/mod.rs\` with shared test infrastructure, then
consolidate all tests into external test files.

**Step 1: Create \`tests/helpers/mod.rs\` with:**
- \`Calls\` struct (currently duplicated in lib.rs:831-847 and integration_suite.rs:34-50)
- \`test_policy()\` (currently duplicated in both files)
- \`request_json()\` — use the integration_suite version (it has session header support)
- \`request_text()\` — use the integration_suite version (it has session header support)
- \`spawn_psp()\` — unified version returning (shutdown_tx, handle, AppState)
- \`spawn_mock_backend()\` — a SINGLE configurable mock backend that replaces
  all seven current spawners. Accept a config/closure or use a builder pattern.
  The current spawners (spawn_backend, spawn_create_backend, spawn_sweep_backend,
  spawn_delete_backend, spawn_inspect_backend, spawn_lifecycle_backend) are 80%
  identical — they all bind TCP, create an axum router with a catch-all, handle
  shutdown. The only difference is the route handler logic.
- \`normalize_versioned_path()\` — DELETE the duplicate in integration_suite.rs,
  import from the crate directly.

**Step 2: Create \`tests/proxy_tests.rs\` with tests migrated from lib.rs:**
- proxies_container_lifecycle_endpoints
- rewrites_container_inspect_host_ports_for_client_connectivity
- injects_session_labels_on_container_create
- startup_sweep_removes_stale_managed_containers
- cleanup_tracked_resources_deletes_containers_on_shutdown
- derives_secret_safe_audit_context
- accepts_versioned_and_unversioned_supported_paths
- denies_policy_blocked_container_create_with_rule_id
- rejects_unsupported_endpoints_with_structured_error

**Step 3: Clean up \`tests/integration_suite.rs\`:**
- Replace inline helpers with \`mod helpers;\` imports
- Remove the duplicated \`normalize_versioned_path()\`
- Remove duplicated \`Calls\`, \`test_policy\`, \`request_json\`, \`request_text\`

**Verification:**
- \`cargo test\` must pass with all tests running
- No test helpers should be duplicated anywhere
- No \`#[cfg(test)]\` blocks in any \`src/*.rs\` file except \`policy.rs\`
  (policy unit tests are fine — they're small and test internal logic)
close_reason: Created tests/helpers/mod.rs with configurable MockHandler trait replacing 7 spawners

title: SEC: Add UsernsMode and CgroupnsMode to namespace checks
description: CRIT-3: policy.rs only checks 4 namespace fields (network, pid, ipc, uts). Add UsernsMode and CgroupnsMode to the host namespace check array. Add tests for both bypasses.
close_reason: done

title: Move hop_by_hop_header from paths.rs to backend.rs
description: BAD-4: hop_by_hop_header has nothing to do with path normalization. Move it to backend.rs where the header filtering actually happens. Update proxy.rs import.
close_reason: done

title: REFACTOR: Extract error.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract all error types and response helpers into \`src/error.rs\`.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`ProxyError\` enum + impl (lines 715-745)
- \`ErrorBody\` struct (lines 748-754)
- \`IntoResponse for ProxyError\` impl (lines 757-804)
- \`json_response()\` helper (lines 807-817)

**Dependencies within the moved code:**
- \`ProxyError\` references \`policy::Denial\` — use \`crate::policy::Denial\`.
- \`json_response\` uses axum + serde — straightforward imports.

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod error;\`
- \`pub use error::ProxyError;\` (preserve public API)

**Target file size:** ~100 lines.
**Test impact:** None — these types have no inline tests.
**No dependencies on other refactoring issues.**
close_reason: Extracted ProxyError, ErrorBody, json_response to error.rs (144 lines)

title: SEC: Unix backend timeout must cover body collection
description: BAD-6: backend.rs Unix backend timeout only covers the response future, not body.collect(). Wrap the entire operation (request + body collection) in a single tokio::time::timeout. Also add response body size limit (CRIT-2) to both backends - cap at 64MiB.
close_reason: Fixed Unix backend timeout to cover body collection and added 64 MiB response body size limit for both backends

title: REFACTOR: Extract audit.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract request audit context into \`src/audit.rs\`.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`RequestAuditContext\` struct + impl (lines 272-296)
- \`operation_name()\` (lines 605-622)
- \`extract_target_image()\` (lines 624-650)
- \`extract_target_container()\` (lines 652-661) — but see note below

**Note on extract_target_container:**
If paths.rs extraction (psp-1tr) has already consolidated
\`container_id_from_path\` and \`extract_target_container\`, then
\`audit.rs\` should call \`crate::paths::container_id_from_path()\` instead
of having its own extraction function.

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod audit;\`

**Target file size:** ~100 lines.
**Test impact:** \`derives_secret_safe_audit_context\` test should move to
\`tests/\` later (TEST-2 issue).
close_reason: Extracted audit.rs (82 lines) with unified container_ref_from_path

title: Remove unsafe libc::getuid, use nix or rustix
description: BAD-5: config.rs uses unsafe { libc::getuid() }. Replace with nix::unistd::getuid() or rustix equivalent. No unsafe needed in a security proxy.
close_reason: done

title: SEC: Fix path traversal in bind mount allowlist validation
description:
**Priority: CRITICAL — this is a security proxy with a security bug.**

`BindMountPolicy::is_allowed()` in `src/policy.rs:184-191` uses prefix matching:
```rust
source.starts_with(&format!("{prefix}/"))
```

A bind mount source of `/workspace/../etc/shadow` passes this check because
`/workspace/..` starts with `/workspace/`. The attacker just mounted `/etc/shadow`.

**Fix:**
- Canonicalize the source path before comparing against the allowlist.
- Use `std::path::Path::components()` to normalize away `..` and `.` segments.
- Do NOT use `std::fs::canonicalize()` — it resolves symlinks and requires the
  path to exist, which it may not at policy-check time. Instead, implement a
  pure lexical normalization (collapse `.`, `..`, remove trailing slashes).
- Add tests for: `../` traversal, `./` no-op, double-slash, trailing slash,
  path that equals the prefix exactly, path that is a sibling with same prefix
  (e.g., `/workspacetmp` must NOT match `/workspace`).

**Files:** `src/policy.rs` only.
**No dependencies on other issues.**
close_reason: Fixed: normalize_bind_path() resolves .. components lexically before prefix check

title: SEC: Streaming body size limit for chunked requests
description: CRIT-1: proxy.rs Content-Length check is bypassed by chunked transfer encoding. Use http_body_util::Limited or stream-count bytes during collection instead of post-hoc size check. Must reject bodies >4MiB even without Content-Length header.
close_reason: done

title: REFACTOR: Extract config.rs from lib.rs
description:
Extract \`Config\` and \`BackendConfig\` into \`src/config.rs\`.

**What to move (from lib.rs):**
- \`Config\` struct + impl (lines 33-74)
- \`BackendConfig\` enum + impl (lines 77-94)

**In lib.rs afterward:**
- \`pub mod config;\`
- \`pub use config::{Config, BackendConfig};\` (preserve public API)

**Target file size:** ~70 lines.
**Test impact:** None — these types have no inline tests.
**No dependencies on other refactoring issues.**
close_reason: Extracted Config and BackendConfig to config.rs (68 lines)

title: SEC: Add request body size limits
description:
`proxy_request()` in `src/lib.rs:336-340` calls `body.collect().await.to_bytes()`
with no size bound. A malicious client can POST a multi-GB payload to
`/containers/create` and the proxy will buffer it all in memory.

**Fix:**
- Add a configurable max body size (default: 4 MiB is generous for container
  create payloads).
- Use axum's `DefaultBodyLimit` layer or check `Content-Length` before collecting.
- Return 413 Payload Too Large with a structured JSON error if exceeded.
- Add a `PSP_MAX_BODY_SIZE` env var to Config.

**Files:** After refactoring, this lives in `proxy.rs` or as tower middleware.
If done before refactoring, modify `proxy_request()` in `lib.rs`.
**No dependencies on other issues — can be done independently.**
close_reason: Added MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES (4MiB) check in proxy_request

title: REFACTOR: Extract proxy.rs and slim lib.rs to re-exports
description:
Final extraction: move the request handler into \`src/proxy.rs\` and reduce
\`lib.rs\` to module declarations, re-exports, \`router()\`, and
\`serve_with_shutdown()\`.

**What to move into \`src/proxy.rs\` (from lib.rs):**
- \`proxy_request()\` async handler (lines 305-429)
- \`maybe_inject_session_labels()\` (lines 583-593)
- \`extract_container_id()\` (lines 595-603)

**What stays in lib.rs:**
- Module declarations (\`pub mod config/error/paths/audit/backend/rewrite/proxy/policy/session\`)
- Re-exports (\`pub use\` for \`Config\`, \`AppState\`, \`serve_with_shutdown\`, etc.)
- \`AppState\` struct + impl (it ties everything together)
- \`router()\` function
- \`serve_with_shutdown()\` function
- \`remove_existing_socket()\` helper
- \`shutdown_signal()\`

**Delete from lib.rs:**
- The entire \`#[cfg(test)] mod tests\` block (653 lines) — tests move in the
  next issue (psp-test-infra).

**After this issue, lib.rs should be ~120 lines with ZERO inline tests.**
Every source file should be under 500 LoC.

**Verification:** Run \`cargo test\` — the inline tests will be gone but
integration_suite.rs should still pass. If it doesn't, fix the imports.
Don't worry about the deleted inline tests yet — they get recreated as
external tests in the next issue.
close_reason: Extracted proxy.rs (173 lines), lib.rs slimmed to 170 lines

title: SEC: Validate session ID from HTTP header
description: CRIT-4: session.rs session_id() accepts arbitrary header values with no validation. Add: max 128 chars, alphanumeric + dash + underscore only. Reject/truncate anything else to prevent label injection and log corruption.
close_reason: Implemented session ID sanitization: max 128 chars, alphanumeric/dash/underscore/dot only, falls back to anonymous
